redundancy_note: Phase 1 uses WHOLE-FILE embeddings; a snippet cannot match its own source file, so 0-redundant is BLINDNESS not cleanliness. True net-new << compile_valid (K.4 recon: ~65% is Joy-of-VEX already in rag/). Needs Phase 3 section-level embeddings to measure.
usable_entries_note: UPPER BOUND only  -  = compile-valid unique, NOT redundancy-filtered. Also includes trivial one-liners + tutorial fragments too atomic/context-bound to be useful standalone RAG entries.
verdict_reasons: only 52.9% compile standalone  -  dominated by context-dependent teaching fragments (vars from prior lines), not self-contained patterns
redundancy vs existing rag/ is UNMEASURED (whole-file embedding blind at snippet granularity); known-high Joy-of-VEX overlap unfiltered
structural_finding: vex-corpus output is a chopped TUTORIAL TRANSCRIPT (sequential fragments that reference vars/context from prior lines  -  Error 1067 the plurality of rejects), NOT a library of standalone VEX patterns. Re-wiring needs vex-corpus to emit self-contained patterns first.
